title: No limit on offline_messages table allows storage exhaustion DoS
description: The offline_messages table (WiredMigrations.swift line 190) has no per-recipient row limit and no maximum total size. The table has an expires_at column, but there is no evidence of a periodic cleanup job or an INSERT-time check that enforces a maximum number of messages per recipient. A malicious user with send_offline_messages privilege can flood the database with messages targeted at any identity, causing disk exhaustion. The ciphertext column is a BLOB with no size limit, compounding the issue.
vulnerable_code:
try db.create(table: "offline_messages", ifNotExists: true) { t in
    t.column("id", .text).primaryKey()
    t.column("sender_identity", .text).notNull()
    t.column("recipient_identity", .text).notNull()
    t.column("ciphertext", .blob).notNull()
    // ... no row count or size constraints
}
recommendation: Add a per-recipient message limit (e.g., 100 messages) enforced at INSERT time. Implement a periodic cleanup job that deletes expired messages (WHERE expires_at < NOW()). Consider adding a maximum ciphertext size check at the application layer.
